Name: The Invisible Man
Author: H.G. Wells
Quote: All men, however highly educated, retain some superstitious inklings.

Name: The Alchemist
Author: Paulo Coelho
Quote: And, when you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you to achieve it.

Name: The Fault in Our Stars
Author: John Green
Quote: I fell in love the way you fall asleep: slowly, and then all at once.

Name: To Kill a Mockingbird
Author: Harper Lee
Quote: Until I feared I would lose it, I never loved to read. One does not love breathing.

Name: The Hitchhiker's Guide to Galaxy
Author: Douglas Adams
Quote: Isn't it enough to see that a garden is beautiful without having to believe that there are fairies at the bottom of it too?

Name: All-Star Superman
Author: Grant Morrison
Quote: You're much stronger than you think you are. Trust me.

Name: Animal Farm
Author: George Orwell
Quote: This work was strictly voluntary, but any animal who absented himself from it would have his rations reduced by half.

Name: Verity
Author: Colleen Hoover
Quote: Most people come to New York to be discovered. The rest of us come here to hide.

Name: Meditations
Author: Marcus Aurelius
Quote: If you are distressed by anything external, the pain is not due to the thing itself, but to your estimate of it.

Name: Peril at the End House
Author: Agatha Christie
Quote: You have a tendency, Hastings, to prefer the least likely. That, no doubt, is from reading too many detective stories.

Name: Murder on the Orient Express
Author: Agatha Christie
Quote: The impossible could not have happened, therefore the impossible must be possible in spite of appearances.

Name: 100 Selected Poems
Author: Emily Dickinson
Quote: Hope is the thing with feathers. That perches in the soul. And sings the tune without the words. And never stops at all.

Name: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd
Author: Agatha Christie
Quote: Oh! money! All the troubles in the world can be put down to money—or the lack of it.

Name: And Then There Were None
Author: Agatha Christie
Quote: I have, let me confess it in all humility, a pitiful human wish that someone should know just how clever I have been

Name: The Great Gatsby
Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
Quote: You see I usually find myself among strangers because I drift here and there trying to forget the sad things that happened to me.

Name: Frankenstein
Author: Mary Shelley
Quote: How mutable are our feelings, and how strange is that clinging love we have of life even in the excess of misery!

Name: Pride & Prejudice
Author: Jane Austen
Quote: It is a truth universally acknowledged, that a single man in possession of a good fortune, must be in want of a wife.

Name: Bhagavad Gita
Author: Vyasa
Quote: Seek refuge in the attitude of detachment and you will amass the wealth of spiritual awareness.

Name: Norwegian Wood
Author: Haruki Murakami
Quote: If you only read the books that everyone else is reading, you can only think what everyone else is thinking.

Name: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
Author: Yuval Noah Harari
Quote: You could never convince a monkey to give you a banana by promising him limitless bananas after death in monkey heaven.

Name: Before the Coffee Gets Cold
Author: Toshikazu Kawaguchi
Quote: I was so absorbed in the things that I couldn’t change, I forgot the most important thing

Name: The Metamorphosis
Author: Franz Kafka
Quote: His growing lack of concern for the others hardly surprised him, whereas previously he had prided himself on being considerate.

Name: The Catcher in the Rye
Author: J.D. Salinger
Quote: What really knocks me out is a book that, when you're all done reading it, you wish the author that wrote it was a terrific friend of yours.

Name: A Man Called Ove
Author: Fredrik Backman
Quote: He went through life with his hands firmly shoved into his pockets. She danced.

Name: Tuesdays with Morrie
Author: Mitch Albom
Quote: It's knowing that your family will be there watching out for you. Nothing else will give you that. Not money. Not fame. Not work.

Name: The Bell Jar
Author: Sylvia Plath
Quote: I took a deep breath and listened to the old brag of my heart: I am, I am, I am.

Name: Man's Search for Meaning
Author: Viktor Frankl
Quote: But there was no need to be ashamed of tears, for tears bore witness that a man had the greatest of courage, the courage to suffer.

Name: The Lightning Thief
Author: Rick Riordan
Quote: It's funny how humans can wrap their mind around things and fit them into their version of reality.

Name: The Sea of Monsters
Author: Rick Riordan
Quote: Tantalus made a wild grab, but the marshmallow committed suicide, diving into the flames.

Name: Titan's Curse
Author: Rick Riordan
Quote: I hoped I wasn't blushing. It was bad enough I had to depend on my mom to drive me to my battles.

Name: The Battle of the Labyrinth
Author: Rick Riordan
Quote: You deal with mythological stuff for a few years, you learn that paradises are usually places where you get killed.

Name: The Last Olympian
Author: Rick Riordan
Quote: The end of the world started when a pegasus landed on the hood of my car.

Name: Brave New World
Author: Aldous Huxley
Quote: But I don't want comfort. I want God, I want poetry, I want real danger, I want freedom, I want goodness. I want sin.

Name: A Thousand Splendid Suns
Author: Khaled Hosseini
Quote: yet love can move people to act in unexpected ways and move them to overcome the most daunting obstacles with startling heroism

Name: Kite Runner
Author: Khaled Hosseini
Quote: And that's the thing about people who mean everything they say. They think everyone else does too.

Name: Six of Crows
Author: Leigh Bardugo
Quote: She'd laughed, and if he could have bottled the sound and gotten drunk on it every night, he would have. It terrified him.

Author: George Orwell
Quote: The Party told you to reject the evidence of your eyes and ears. It was their final, most essential command

Name: The Book Thief
Author: Markus Zusak
Quote: If only she could be so oblivious again, to feel such love without knowing it, mistaking it for laughter.

Name: Life of Pi
Author: Yann Martel
Quote: It is true that those we meet can change us, sometimes so profoundly that we are not the same afterwards, even unto our names.

Name: The Hunger Games
Author: Suzanne Collins
Quote: Kind people have a way of working their way inside me and rooting there.

Name: My Experiments with Truth
Author: Mahatma Gandhi
Quote: Remember that all through history, there have been tyrants and murderers, and for a time, they seem invincible. But in the end, they always fall.

Name: Norse Mythology
Author: Neil Gaiman
Quote: 'Because,' said Thor, 'when something goes wrong, the first thing I always think is, it is Loki’s fault. It saves a lot of time.'

Name: The Shining
Author: Stephen King
Quote: Tough old world, baby. If you're not bolted together tightly, you're gonna shake, rattle, and roll before you turn thirty.

Name: The Philosopher's Stone
Author: J.K. Rowling
Quote: It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live.

Name: The Book of Joy
Author: Douglas Abrams
Quote: Marriages, even the best ones—perhaps especially the best ones—are an ongoing process of spoken and unspoken forgiveness.

Name: The Chamber of Secrets
Author: J.K. Rowling
Quote: It is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.

Name: The Vegetarian
Author: Han Kang
Quote: The feeling that she had never really lived in this world caught her by surprise.

Name: Looking For Alaska
Author: John Green
Quote: Thomas Edison's last words were 'It's very beautiful over there'. I don't know where there is, but I believe it's somewhere, and I hope it's beautiful.

Name: Prisoner of Azkaban
Author: J.K. Rowling
Quote: Happiness can be found even in the darkest of times, if one only remembers to turn on the light.

Name: The Goblet of Fire
Author: J.K. Rowling
Quote: If you want to know what a man's like, take a good look at how he treats his inferiors, not his equals.

Name: Order of the Phoenix
Author: J.K. Rowling
Quote: We've all got both light and dark inside us. What matters is the part we choose to act on.

Name: The Half-Blood Prince
Author: J.K. Rowling
Quote: Do not pity the dead, Harry. Pity the living, and, above all, those who live without love.

Name: The Deathly Hallows
Author: J.K. Rowling
Quote: Of course it is happening inside your head, Harry, but why on earth should that mean that it is not real?

Name: Think Like a Monk
Author: Jay Shetty
Quote: When we accept the temporary nature of everything in our lives, we can feel gratitude for the good fortune of getting to borrow them for a time.

Name: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
Author: Eric Jorgenson
Quote: The three big ones in life are wealth, health, and happiness. We pursue them in that order, but their importance is reverse.

Name: Men Without Women
Author: Haruki Murakami
Quote: The scene seemed somehow divorced from reality, although reality, he knew, could at times be terribly unreal.

Name: Eat Pray Love
Author: Elizabeth Gilbert
Quote: This is a good sign, having a broken heart. It means we have tried for something.

Name: Four Thousand Weeks
Author: Oliver Burkeman
Quote: Mortality makes it impossible to ignore the absurdity of living solely for the future.

Name: Pachinko
Author: Min Jin Lee
Quote: History has failed us, but no matter.

Name: The Song of Achilles
Author: Madeline Miller
Quote: I could recognize him by touch alone, by smell; I would know him blind, by the way his breaths came and his feet struck the earth.

Name: Thinking, Fast and Slow
Author: Daniel Kahneman
Quote: Nothing in life is as important as you think it is while you are thinking about it.
